events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: VFDs & motor controls, rough-complete stage, observed condition is "missing load reactor on long motor leads." This is a defect/non-compliance issue. Long motor leads between VFD and motor require a load reactor (or dV/dt filter/sine filter) to protect motor insulation from reflected wave voltage spikes. Missing it is a defect.

Decision: fail (non-compliant, potential hazard to motor).
Risk: high (could damage motor, cause failure).
Work stage: rough-complete.
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant.
Percent complete: maybe 85.
Value: 0 (no numeric reading asked).
Confidence: 0.9.
Findings: missing load reactor on long motor leads; VFD output to motor exceeds manufacturer's maximum cable length without reactor.
Actions: install appropriately sized load reactor/dV/dt filter per mfr manual before energizing.
Rationale: long leads cause reflected wave voltage spikes damaging motor insulation; mfr manuals and NEC 430 require proper motor protection.
References: NEC 430, mfr manuals.
